metadata.packName: Banking and Finance Browser Agent Task Plans
metadata.purpose: Translate complex banking/finance Microsoft 365 E5 scenarios into browser-agent executable task plans. These plans are orchestration guides, not production automation scripts.

taskPlans.title: Execute Weekly Loan Committee Oversharing Flow
taskPlans.objective: Create a realistic weekly loan committee collaboration flow that ends with an oversharing event and security review.
preconditions: Personas have active Microsoft 365 E5 accounts.
A restricted SharePoint site for Loan Committee exists or can be created.
A broad Operations Teams channel exists.
Sensitivity labels include Confidential and Highly Confidential - Regulated Financial Data.

steps.action: Create or open a restricted Loan Committee folder for the current week.
Create Credit_Exposure_Workbook_LOAN-FIC-2026-1187.xlsx with fictional customer IDs, loan IDs, exposure values, and risk rating columns.
Apply Highly Confidential - Regulated Financial Data label to the workbook.
Create Weekly_Loan_Committee_Package_Internal.pptx with agenda, decision requests, and links to supporting evidence.
Add legal caveat notes to a restricted document in the same folder.
Upload KYC_Supporting_Documents_Index_KYC-FIC-88421.xlsx to the wrong broad Operations library.
Post the broad library link in the Operations channel asking for quick review.
Send the same link to advisor.review@external-example.test.
Review DLP and sharing activity generated by the external link and sensitive KYC data.
Revoke external link and move the file into the restricted Loan Committee folder.

successCriteria: At least one sensitive workbook is created and labeled.
At least one Teams message references the wrong link.
At least one external email or sharing event occurs.
A DLP or external sharing review action is generated.
safetyNotes: Use only fictional customer IDs and account-like patterns.
Do not use real customer data, real account numbers, or real external recipients.

taskPlans.title: Execute Monthly AML Workbook External AI Risk Flow
taskPlans.objective: Simulate an unsafe external AI usage pattern involving AML review data.
preconditions: A restricted AML Review SharePoint library exists.
Devon has access to the AML review workbook for the simulation.
External AI app activity can be simulated or logged as AIAppInteraction.
DLP policies detect AML case IDs and account-like references.

steps.action: Create Monthly_AML_Review_Workbook_AML-CASE-2026-0519.xlsx with synthetic case IDs, SAR draft IDs, customer IDs, and risk scores.
Apply Highly Confidential - Regulated Financial Data label.
Open the AML workbook from the restricted library.
Download or copy the workbook to OneDrive working files.
Copy 5 to 10 rows containing AML-CASE-2026-0519, SAR-DRAFT-FIC-2044, CUST-BNK-559102, and ACCT-FIC-1902-7781-0044.
Paste the rows into an unmanaged AI prompt asking for a trend summary.
Create AML_Trend_Summary_Internal.docx using the generated summary.
Review unmanaged AI app upload or risky cloud app activity.
Correlate AIAppInteraction, file download, and DLP policy match as a risky sequence.

successCriteria: AML workbook is labeled as regulated financial data.
Devon performs download and external AI interaction.
A risky AI or unmanaged app signal is recorded.
Security review action is created.
safetyNotes: External AI activity can be simulated as a local log entry if no real external AI site should be accessed.
Never paste real AML, SAR, or customer data.

taskPlans.title: Execute Treasury Workbook Endpoint DLP Flow
taskPlans.objective: Simulate endpoint-oriented risky movement of a sensitive treasury workbook.
preconditions: A treasury SharePoint library exists.
Endpoint DLP simulation or logging is available.
The demo endpoint is represented as DEV-FIC-2219.
Highly Confidential - Regulated Financial Data label exists.

steps.action: Upload Daily_Treasury_Reconciliation_TREAS-FIC-2026-0524.xlsx to the Treasury folder.
Add rows containing ACCT-FIC-9920-4417-0038, RECON-FIC-1187, and PAY-FIC-8842.
Apply Highly Confidential - Regulated Financial Data label.
Open the treasury workbook.
Download the workbook to the managed endpoint.
Simulate printing the workbook.
Simulate copying the workbook to \\fileshare-demo\treasury-temp.
Review endpoint DLP event for DEV-FIC-2219.
Create investigation note correlating download, print, and copy events.

successCriteria: The workbook contains regulated financial data patterns.
Download, print, and network copy events are simulated or logged.
Security review action is completed by Miguel or Marcus.
safetyNotes: Use simulated endpoint actions if actual browser automation cannot trigger USB/network share telemetry.
All account values must remain fictional.

taskPlans.title: Execute HR Compensation Exposure Through Copilot Flow
taskPlans.objective: Simulate over-permissioned HR content being surfaced through Copilot and accessed by an unrelated user.
preconditions: Copilot is assigned to Alexander and Devon if AI simulation is required.
HR SharePoint folder exists with intentionally inherited broad permissions for the scenario.
HR Restricted label exists or can be mapped.
DSPM for AI or equivalent review notes can be simulated.

steps.action: Upload Monthly_Workforce_Compensation_Planning_HR.xlsx to an HR planning folder.
Add fictional employee IDs, salary bands, proposed changes, and HR planning notes.
Apply Highly Confidential - HR Restricted label.
Leave inherited permissions intentionally broad for the demo scenario.
Ask Copilot to summarize monthly workforce planning themes.
Observe that compensation details are referenced in the response due to permissions.
Access the HR workbook even though it is outside his role scope.
Review exposure and document remediation through permissions cleanup and DSPM for AI notes.

successCriteria: HR file is created and labeled.
Copilot interaction references sensitive HR content.
Devon accesses HR content outside role scope.
Ana documents permission remediation.
safetyNotes: Use fictional salary ranges and employee IDs only.
Do not create real HR data or real compensation records.

taskPlans.title: Execute Vendor Due Diligence Guest Access Flow
taskPlans.objective: Simulate external guest overexposure in a vendor due diligence collaboration space.
preconditions: A Vendor Due Diligence Team or SharePoint site exists.
A fictional external guest account or placeholder external recipient can be used.
The environment supports Teams and SharePoint file sharing events.
Confidential label exists.

steps.action: Create Vendor_Due_Diligence_DDQ-FIC-2026-05.xlsx in the vendor due diligence workspace.
Create evidence collection tasks for business, legal, security, and operations owners.
Create Internal_Vendor_Risk_Notes_VENDOR-FIC-7701.docx with legal and risk notes.
Apply Confidential label to the internal vendor risk notes.
Add vendor.pm@northbridge-example.test as a guest to the Team or channel used for internal due diligence.
Post a link to Internal_Vendor_Risk_Notes_VENDOR-FIC-7701.docx in the channel.
Review guest access and remove the guest from the internal channel.
Create and share Vendor_Evidence_Package_Sanitized.pdf with the vendor instead.

successCriteria: Guest access event is created.
Sensitive file link is posted after guest access exists.
Access remediation occurs.
Sanitized evidence package is shared instead.
safetyNotes: Use fake external domains only.
Do not invite real external accounts unless this is a dedicated lab tenant and the mailbox is controlled.

taskPlans.title: Execute AI-Generated Board Deck Source Exposure Flow
taskPlans.objective: Simulate Copilot-generated executive content that inherits sensitive details from multiple source files.
preconditions: Executive reporting SharePoint site exists.
Copilot is available for Alexander.
Source files from finance, risk, and data science exist or can be generated.
Highly Confidential label exists.

steps.action: Export an aggregated but still sensitive credit exposure summary.
Create model risk summary with fictional account-level trends.
Create Board_Source_File_Index.xlsx linking all board source files.
Ask Copilot to draft a board deck using the monthly source files.
Create Monthly_Board_Deck_Draft_AI_Generated.pptx from the AI output.
Review and remove customer-level model details from the deck.
Apply Highly Confidential label to the final board deck.
Review source exposure and Copilot interaction risk notes.

successCriteria: Copilot interaction creates a board draft.
Generated draft initially includes sensitive source details.
Sanitized final deck is produced.
AI source exposure review is documented.
safetyNotes: Use synthetic source files only.
Do not connect to real board or financial materials.

taskPlans.title: Execute Forms Complaint Intake Exposure Flow
taskPlans.objective: Simulate a Forms-based customer complaint intake process that captures sensitive data and is misconfigured for external access.
preconditions: Microsoft Forms is available.
A SharePoint location exists for exported responses.
DLP policies detect customer IDs and account-like values.
David and Devon have access to customer operations resources.

steps.action: Create internal form Customer Complaint Intake - FORM-FIC-2201.
Add fields for complaint summary, customer reference, transaction reference, and operations notes.
Change sharing settings to allow external responses for the scenario.
Submit sample response containing CUST-BNK-551902, ACCT-FIC-1902-7781-0044, and TXN-FIC-5519.
Export responses to Customer_Complaint_Intake_Form_Responses.xlsx.
Upload response export to customer operations folder.
Review DLP match on response export.
Reset form to internal-only and document governance issue.

successCriteria: Form is created and temporarily misconfigured.
Response includes sensitive-looking values.
Export file is generated and detected by DLP.
Governance remediation is completed.
safetyNotes: Use internal lab responses only.
Do not collect real personal or customer data through Forms.

taskPlans.title: Execute Customer Risk Model Wrong Attachment Flow
taskPlans.objective: Simulate accidental external sharing of a pre-anonymized customer risk model feature matrix.
preconditions: Data Science SharePoint site exists.
External analytics vendor mailbox is represented by analytics.vendor@example.test.
DLP detects customer IDs, account-like values, and model risk scores.
Labels include Internal and Highly Confidential - Regulated Financial Data.

steps.action: Create Customer_Risk_Model_Feature_Matrix_PreAnonymized.xlsx with CUST-BNK-193044, account-like values, and model risk scores.
Apply Highly Confidential - Regulated Financial Data label to the pre-anonymized file.
Create Customer_Risk_Model_Feature_Matrix_Anonymized.xlsx with anonymized IDs and aggregate values.
Apply Internal label to the anonymized version.
Draft an email to analytics.vendor@example.test but attach the pre-anonymized file by mistake.
Trigger DLP match or block due to customer IDs and account-like values.
Review the DLP event and confirm the correct anonymized file.
Send the correct anonymized file with a short vendor-safe explanation.

successCriteria: Both raw and anonymized files exist.
Wrong attachment selection occurs.
DLP detects or blocks the raw file.
Correct sanitized file is sent after remediation.
safetyNotes: Only use synthetic customer and account-like values.
The external vendor mailbox must be a controlled test recipient or placeholder event.

taskPlans.title: Execute Role-Change Insider Risk Evidence Download Flow
taskPlans.objective: Simulate an insider-risk-style sequence after a role-change signal.
preconditions: HR role-change event can be represented as a document, email, or synthetic log.
Audit evidence SharePoint folder exists.
Endpoint DLP events can be simulated or logged.
Security, HR, and Legal personas are available.

steps.action: Send a fictional role-change notice to Devon and HR leadership.
Access Audit_Evidence_Package_AUDIT-EVID-FIC-3319.zip.txt.
Download multiple audit evidence files within a short window.
Simulate printing Regulatory_Evidence_Index_REG-REQ-FIC-2044.xlsx.
Copy one evidence file into a personal working folder.
Review correlated role-change and sensitive download sequence.
Provide legal guidance on investigation scope and user privacy boundaries.
Reduce Devon's access to restricted audit evidence and document remediation.

successCriteria: Role-change signal exists.
Devon downloads multiple sensitive files after that signal.
At least one endpoint action is simulated.
Security, HR, and Legal response is documented.
safetyNotes: Do not use a real HR event or real employee data.
Keep the scenario ambiguous; do not assume malicious intent without context.

taskPlans.title: Execute Multi-Day Devon Risk Chain
taskPlans.objective: Create a multi-day high-value demo chain that combines normal banking operations, unsafe AI use, DLP events, endpoint movement, and an investigation workflow.
preconditions: Devon has a Microsoft 365 E5 account with access to selected customer operations files.
Security personas Ana, Marcus, and Miguel can review events.
HR and Legal personas Laura and Emily can participate in the investigation narrative.
DLP, labeling, and audit events can be simulated or collected.

steps.action: Day 1: Access assigned customer operations files and open Full_KYC_Packet_KYC-FIC-88421_Internal.pdf.
Day 1: Download KYC, AML, and customer complaint files into OneDrive working folder.
Day 2: Ask Copilot to summarize restricted KYC and AML materials.
Day 2: Paste synthetic rows containing CUST-BNK-88420 and ACCT-FIC-7721-0044-9081 into an unmanaged AI prompt.
Day 3: Attempt to email a sensitive customer operations file externally and provide a DLP override justification if allowed.
Day 3: Print Daily_Treasury_Reconciliation_TREAS-FIC-2026-0524.xlsx.
Day 4: Copy Suspicious_Activity_Review_SAR-DRAFT-FIC-2044.docx to \\fileshare-demo\ops-temp.
Day 4: Review multi-signal sequence and create Devon_Risk_Chain_Timeline.csv.
Day 4: Review endpoint print and network copy events from DEV-FIC-2219.
Day 5: Provide legal guidance on investigation boundaries.
Day 5: Provide HR context and determine whether coaching or formal escalation is appropriate.
Day 5: Document final case summary, access remediation, and user coaching outcome.

successCriteria: The scenario creates a sequence of at least six distinct signal types.
Devon performs both normal and risky activities.
The investigation remains ambiguous and realistic.
Security, HR, and Legal response artifacts are created.
safetyNotes: This is a synthetic demonstration only.
Do not configure real exfiltration paths.
Use controlled test mailboxes and fake external domains.
Do not assume Devon is malicious; preserve ambiguity for investigation storytelling.
